tool.title: Projectile Motion with UQ (rappture2web)
tool.about:
Classic projectile motion example with Uncertainty Quantification.
Set any of the numeric inputs to a uniform or gaussian distribution to explore how parameter uncertainty propagates through the simulation. The 'Number of time steps' input has UQ disabled — it always uses an exact value.

about.label: Initial Height
about.description: The initial height of the projectile above the ground.

about.label: Initial Velocity
about.description: The initial speed of the projectile.

about.label: Launch Angle
about.description: The angle (in degrees) at which the projectile is launched.

about.label: Gravity (g)
about.description: Gravitational acceleration. Standard gravity is 9.80665 m/s^2.

about.label: Number of time steps
about.description: How many points to use when plotting the trajectory.

about.label: Trajectory
about.description: Distance vs. height of the projectile.

about.label: Height vs Time
about.description: Height of the projectile as a function of time.

about.label: Maximum Height
about.description: Peak height reached by the projectile.
